that in the lOth, and the figure in the I2th is less than that in
the llth. and lastly the figure in the Lagna is greater than that
in the 12th house. In our example the Lagna has 23 dots, 12th
house 28, llth 33 and 10th 25. Here all the conditions except
the last one are fulfilled. So though he may be a spend-thrift
yet he will have plenty of money and enjoyments. In the
Sarvastakavarga chart you make three divisions of four Rasis
each commencing from Pisces. These three divisions repesent
the three stapes of life of the subject. That part of life which
has the maximum number of benefic dots will prove to be the
happiest one. In our example chart the three groups have
respectively 110, 113. and 114. Hence we have to conclude that
the last period of life will be the happiest. According to another
view the grouping must be done not with Pisces as the starting
R&Ji, but with the one that happens to be the 12th from the

ASTAKAVARGA 285
Lagna RaSi.
